topic: Sync deal checklist — what to negotiate

slides.eyebrow: Education · Sync deal checklist — what to negotiate
slides.headline: You got the offer.
Here's why that's the most expensive reaction in sync. ↓
slides.lede: Your first instinct is to say yes immediately.

slides.headline: When a placement offer lands, you have exactly one negotiation window.
slides.lede: Before you sign, here's what experienced composers always check:

For branded content: fees typically run 20–40% higher than the first offer.
For streaming originals: push for the stated upper range.
Ask once. Clearly. Most supervisors expect it.

slides.headline:
EXCLUSIVITY PERIOD
"Exclusive" locks your track for a window — no other placements during that time.
slides.lede:
Six months: acceptable.
Two years: 3× the standard rate.
Negotiate the window before you negotiate the fee.

slides.headline:
TERRITORY
UK-only and global use are fundamentally different deals.
slides.lede:
A global campaign should cost 3–5× a regional one.
If the territory isn't defined in the offer: define it before you respond.

slides.headline:
TERM LENGTH
One-off vs.
slides.lede:
rolling vs. in-perpetuity are three different contracts.
In-perpetuity means they can use your track forever.
That has a price significantly above the standard placement fee.

slides.headline:
PERFORMANCE ROYALTIES
These are separate from your upfront fee.
slides.lede:
Every time the content airs anywhere, royalties generate through your PRO.
Non-negotiable prerequisite: register before any placement is signed.
You cannot collect these retroactively.

slides.headline: The sync fee is the headline number.
slides.lede: Performance royalties are the compounding income beneath it. · Know both before you sign anything.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
